audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for laborers in vineyard, first/last reversal, third passion prediction, mother of Zebedee sons, cup, servant/slave greatness, ransom language, and healing of two blind men.

verses.restored: For the reign of the heavens is like a householder who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
Agreeing with the workers for a denarius for the day, he sent them into his vineyard.
Going out about the third hour, he saw others standing idle in the marketplace.
He said to them, You also go into the vineyard, and whatever is right/just I will give you.
So they went. Again going out about the sixth and ninth hour, he did similarly.
About the eleventh hour he went out and found others standing, and said, Why have you stood here all day idle?
They said to him, Because no one hired us. He said, You also go into the vineyard.
When evening came, the lord of the vineyard said to his manager, Call the workers and give them the wage, beginning from the last to the first.
Those hired about the eleventh hour came and received a denarius each.
When the first came, they supposed they would receive more, but they also received a denarius each.
Receiving it, they murmured against the householder,
saying, These last worked one hour, and you made them equal to us who bore the burden of the day and the scorching heat.
He answered one of them, Friend, I do you no injustice. Did you not agree with me for a denarius?
Take what is yours and go. I wish to give to this last one as also to you.
Is it not lawful for me to do what I wish with what is mine? Or is your eye harmful because I am good/generous?
So the last will be first, and the first last.
As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the twelve disciples aside privately and on the way said to them,
Look, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Humanity will be handed over to the chief priests and scribes, and they will condemn him to death.
They will hand him over to the nations to mock, flog, and crucify; and on the third day he will be raised.
Then the mother of the sons of Zebedee came to him with her sons, bowing and asking something from him.
He said to her, What do you want? She said, Say that these two sons of mine may sit, one at your right and one at your left, in your reign.
Jesus answered, You do not know what you ask. Are you able to drink the cup that I am about to drink? They said, We are able.
He said to them, My cup you will drink; but to sit at my right and left is not mine to give, except to those for whom it has been prepared by my Father.
When the ten heard, they were indignant about the two brothers.
Jesus called them and said, You know that the rulers of the nations lord it over them, and the great exercise authority over them.
It shall not be so among you; but whoever wishes to become great among you shall be your servant.
Whoever wishes to be first among you shall be your slave,
just as the Son of Humanity did not come to be served but to serve, and to give his life/soul as a ransom for many.
As they were going out from Jericho, a great crowd followed him.
And look, two blind men sitting beside the way, hearing that Jesus was passing by, cried out, Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David.
The crowd rebuked them so they would be silent, but they cried out even more, Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David.
Jesus stood still, called them, and said, What do you want me to do for you?
They said to him, Lord, that our eyes may be opened.
Moved with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes; immediately they received sight and followed him.
